title: Cephaelis mannii
taxonomy: Cephaelis mannii is classified within the family Rubiaceae, a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This classification places Cephaelis mannii in the order Gentianales, which comprises several other flowering plant families. Further, Cephaelis mannii can be classified as a member of the genus Cephaelis, which contains approximately 300 species of flowering plants. The genus Cephaelis is part of the tribe Palicoureeae, a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Cephaelis mannii's classification provides insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Cephaelis mannii has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal uses have not been extensively scientifically studied, but its traditional applications suggest potential benefits for human health. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

constituents: Cephaelis mannii contains various active constituents, including caffeine and other xanthines. The plant is a source of several alkaloids, such as cephaelidine and cephaeline. intro: Cephaelis mannii, commonly known as wild coffee or Mann's wild coffee, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, act as an anti-inflammatory, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis mannii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cephaelis mannii is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into folk remedies for various health conditions.
